**Investigating comment wrapping rules**

I need to address the issue with ktlint's standard rule for comment wrapping, specifically for comments within single-line lambda literals. I'm trying to identify if it's the CommentWrappingRule or WrappingRule that's causing block comments to wrap to multiline when they shouldn't. It seems that the input `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` is currently changing to multiline, which isn't ideal. I'll have to inspect the CommentWrappingRule in detail to understand its behavior.

 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}
128→
129→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId
130→

132→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135→//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
138→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139→        }
140→    }

142→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
143→        node: ASTNode,
144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148→
149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151→            return
152→        }
153→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156→        val wrapBlock =
157→            when {
158→                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159→                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160→                    false
161→                }
162→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166→                }
167→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168→                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179→                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180→                }
181→                else -> false
182→            }
183→        if (wrapBlock) {
184→            startOfBlock
185→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189→                        autoCorrect,
190→                        emit,
191→                    )
192→                }
193→        }
194→    }

196→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
197→        node: ASTNode,
198→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
199→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
200→    ) {
201→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
202→        var newlineInBetween = false
203→        var parameterListInBetween = false
204→        var numberOfArgs = 0
205→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
206→        // matching ), ] or }
207→        val r = node.nextSibling {
208→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
209→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
210→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
211→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
212→            if (isValueArgument) {
213→                numberOfArgs++
214→                firstArg = it
215→            }
216→            it.elementType == rElementType
217→        }!!
218→        if (
219→            !newlineInBetween ||
220→            // keep { p ->
221→            // }
222→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
223→            // keep ({
224→            // }) and (object : C {
225→            // })
226→            (
227→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
228→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
229→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
230→                )
231→        ) {
232→            return
233→        }
234→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
235→            // keep:
236→            // for (foo in listOf(
237→            //     "foo-1",
238→            //     "foo-2"
239→            // )) { ... }
240→            // but reject:
241→            // for (
242→            //     foo in listOf(
243→            //         "foo-1",
244→            //         "foo-2"
245→            //     )
246→            // ) { ... }
247→            return
248→        }
249→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
250→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
251→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
252→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
253→                    it.textLength > 0
254→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
255→            // IDEA quirk:
256→            // if (true &&
257→            //     true
258→            // ) {
259→            // }
260→            // instead of
261→            // if (
262→            //     true &&
263→            //     true
264→            // ) {
265→            // }
266→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
267→        ) {
268→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
269→        }
270→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
271→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.indent())
272→        }
273→    }

275→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
276→        node: ASTNode,
277→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
278→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
279→    ) {
280→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
281→        if (
282→            node.textContains('\n') &&
283→            entries.size > 1 &&
284→            // e.g.
285→            //
286→            // class A : B, C,
287→            //     D
288→            // or
289→            // class A : B, C({
290→            // }), D
291→            //
292→            // but not
293→            //
294→            // class A : B, C, D({
295→            // })
296→            !(
297→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
298→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
299→                )
300→        ) {
301→            // put space after :
302→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
303→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
304→                if (
305→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
306→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
307→                ) {
308→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
309→                }
310→            }
311→            // put entries on separate lines
312→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
313→            for (c in node.children()) {
314→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
315→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
316→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
317→                ) {
318→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
319→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
320→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
321→                        emit = emit,
322→                        indent = node.indent(),
323→                    )
324→                }
325→            }
326→        }
327→    }

329→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
330→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
331→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true

333→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
334→        node: ASTNode,
335→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
336→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
337→    ) {
338→        for (c in node.children()) {
339→            val hasLineBreak =
340→                when (c.elementType) {
341→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
342→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
343→                    else -> false
344→                }
345→            if (hasLineBreak) {
346→                // rearrange
347→                //
348→                // a, b, value(
349→                // ), c, d
350→                //
351→                // to
352→                //
353→                // a, b,
354→                // value(
355→                // ),
356→                // c, d
357→
358→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
359→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
360→                if (
361→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
362→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
363→                ) {
364→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
365→                }
366→                // insert \n after multi-line value
367→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
368→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
369→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
370→                if (
371→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
372→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
373→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
374→                    // value(
375→                    // ), // a comment
376→                    // c, d
377→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
378→                ) {
379→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
380→                }
381→            }
382→        }
383→    }

385→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
386→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
387→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }

389→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
392→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
393→    ) {
394→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
395→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
396→            node
397→                .children()
398→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
399→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
400→                    typeProjection
401→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
402→                        .let { prevSibling ->
403→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
404→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
405→                                if (autoCorrect) {
406→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
407→                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
408→                                    )
409→                                }
410→                            }
411→                        }
412→                }
413→
414→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
415→            node
416→                .findChildByType(GT)
417→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
418→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
419→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
420→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
421→                        if (autoCorrect) {
422→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
423→                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }

433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458→                    true,
459→                )
460→                if (autoCorrect) {
461→                    node as LeafPsiElement
462→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463→                }
464→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465→            }
466→    }

468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }

495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }

535→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
536→        node: ASTNode,
537→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539→        indent: String,
540→    ) {
541→        emit(
542→            node.startOffset - 1,
543→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
544→            true,
545→        )
546→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
547→        if (autoCorrect) {
548→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
549→        }

552→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
553→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
554→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
555→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
556→        indent: String? = null,
557→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
558→    ) {
559→        emit(
560→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
561→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
562→            true,
563→        )
564→        LOGGER.trace {
565→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
566→        }
567→        if (autoCorrect) {
568→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
569→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
570→        }
571→    }

573→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
574→        for (child in node.children()) {
575→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
576→                val v = child.text
577→                if (v == "\n") {
578→                    return true
579→                }
580→            }
581→        }

585→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
586→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
587→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
588→            textContains('\n')
589→        } else {
590→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
591→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
592→        }

603→    /**
604→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
605→     *     for (foo in listOf(
606→     *         "foo-1",
607→     *         "foo-2"
608→     *     )) { ... }
609→     * but reject:
610→     *     for (
611→     *         foo in listOf(
612→     *             "foo-1",
613→     *             "foo-2"
614→     *         )
615→     *     ) { ... }

617→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
618→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
619→            return false
620→        }
621→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
622→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
623→        }
624→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
625→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
626→        }
627→
628→        var node: ASTNode? = this
629→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
630→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
631→                return false
632→            }
633→            node = node.nextSibling()
634→        }
635→        return true
636→    }

638→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
639→        node: ASTNode,
640→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
641→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
642→    ) {
643→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
644→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
645→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
646→                return
647→            }
648→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
649→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
650→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
651→            val wrapBlock =
652→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
653→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
654→                    )
655→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
656→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
657→                    endOfBlock,
658→                    autoCorrect,
659→                    emit,
660→                    node.treeParent.indent(),
661→                )
662→            }
663→        }
664→    }

666→    private companion object {
667→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
668→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
669→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
670→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
671→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
672→            ).toMap()
673→    }

**Clarifying code comments**

It looks like I’m trying to understand how comments should be handled in code structures. Non-indented comments on the same line as a preceding block should perhaps be treated differently, particularly if there's whitespace involved. The goal is to ignore comments that are strictly between braces and whitespace, specifically in the pattern LBRACE WS BLOCK_COMMENT WS RBRACE. This might also apply to various brace types in different coding situations, like Kotlin's block structures with single-line comments. I'll implement this in the CommentWrappingRule.**Implementing a comment detection helper**

I want to create a helper function, `isBlockCommentInsideSingleLineBlockOnlyContent()`, for detecting block comments. The function will need to traverse the Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) around a block comment node. Specifically, I’ll check two things: if the nearest non-whitespace leaf before the comment is `{` and if the nearest non-whitespace leaf after is `}` without any newlines in between. If they’re on the same line, I’ll ignore it to avoid unnecessary emissions. It's important to ensure the wrapping rules aren’t miscalculating line lengths due to the presence of comments.**Exploring comment wrapping rules**

It seems the goal is to keep single-line blocks intact even when they contain block comments. The wrapping rule uses maxLineLength to determine when to wrap lines, which might lead to unwanted newlines if the length exceeds 100. I suspect other rules are splitting lines due to the assumption that block comments shouldn't be in single-line blocks. To address this, I'll run ktlint and implement a fix along with new tests while ensuring not to modify the existing test files. I’ll create a new test file, `CommentWrappingRuleSingleLineBlockTest.kt`, and first inspect the current tests for the right structure.

358→ * assertions operate on the same state.

360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**

367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.

370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId

373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.

377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )

594→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
595→        val ruleProviders =

598→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.

603→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)

657→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =

668→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }

content.text:
Implemented a targeted exception in `CommentWrappingRule` to ignore a block comment when it’s the only element inside a **single-line `{ ... }` block** (e.g. `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`). This prevents ktlint from reporting/wrapping it as an invalid “block comment in between other elements”.

Added a new unit test `CommentWrappingRuleSingleLineBlockTest` to verify the snippet remains unchanged and produces no lint violations.

 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117→    ) {
118→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
119→        if (autoCorrect) {
120→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
127→}
128→
129→public val COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = CommentWrappingRule().ruleId

145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,

149→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {

169→                        node
170→                            .leaves(false)
171→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173→                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174→                        node
175→                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176→                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177→                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178→                            .sumOf { it.textLength }

297→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
298→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
299→                )

301→            // put space after :
302→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
303→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!

305→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
306→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
307→                ) {
308→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
309→                }
310→            }
311→            // put entries on separate lines
312→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
313→            for (c in node.children()) {

315→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&

333→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
334→        node: ASTNode,
335→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
336→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
337→    ) {
338→        for (c in node.children()) {
339→            val hasLineBreak =
340→                when (c.elementType) {
341→                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
342→                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
343→                    else -> false
344→                }
345→            if (hasLineBreak) {
346→                // rearrange
347→                //
348→                // a, b, value(
349→                // ), c, d
350→                //
351→                // to
352→                //
353→                // a, b,
354→                // value(
355→                // ),
356→                // c, d
357→
358→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
359→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
360→                if (
361→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
362→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
363→                ) {
364→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
365→                }
366→                // insert \n after multi-line value
367→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
368→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
369→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
370→                if (
371→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
372→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
373→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
374→                    // value(
375→                    // ), // a comment
376→                    // c, d
377→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
378→                ) {
379→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
380→                }
381→            }
382→        }
383→    }

389→    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
392→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
393→    ) {
394→        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
395→            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
396→            node
397→                .children()
398→                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
399→                .forEach { typeProjection ->
400→                    typeProjection
401→                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
402→                        .let { prevSibling ->
403→                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
404→                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
405→                                if (autoCorrect) {
406→                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
407→                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
408→                                    )
409→                                }
410→                            }
411→                        }
412→                }
413→
414→            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
415→            node
416→                .findChildByType(GT)
417→                ?.let { closingAngle ->
418→                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
419→                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
420→                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
421→                        if (autoCorrect) {
422→                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
423→                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→                }
428→
429→            Unit
430→        }
431→    }

433→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434→        node: ASTNode,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437→    ) {
438→        node
439→            .treeParent
440→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445→            ?.let {
446→                // rewriting
447→                // """
448→                //     text
449→                // _""".trimIndent()
450→                // to
451→                // """
452→                //     text
453→                // _
454→                // """.trimIndent()
455→                emit(
456→                    node.startOffset,
457→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458→                    true,
459→                )
460→                if (autoCorrect) {
461→                    node as LeafPsiElement
462→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))

464→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }

468→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470→        // if token is in lTokenSet
471→        //     find matching rToken
472→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473→        // return false
474→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477→            // special cases:
478→            // x = y.f({ z ->
479→            // })
480→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481→            // 2, 3)
482→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483→        }
484→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488→        }
489→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490→            return true
491→        }
492→        return false
493→    }

495→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496→        node: ASTNode,
497→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499→    ) {
500→        val p = node.treeParent
501→        if (
502→            // check
503→            // `{ p -> ... }`
504→            // and
505→            // `when { m -> ... }`
506→            // only
507→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510→            // permit
511→            // when {
512→            //     m -> 0 + d({
513→            //     })
514→            // }
515→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516→            // permit
517→            // when (this) {
518→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520→            //     -> true
521→            // }
522→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523→        ) {
524→            return
525→        }
526→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528→        }
529→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532→        }
533→    }

603→    /**
604→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
605→     *     for (foo in listOf(
606→     *         "foo-1",
607→     *         "foo-2"
608→     *     )) { ... }
609→     * but reject:
610→     *     for (

612→     *             "foo-1",

614→     *         )
615→     *     ) { ... }

617→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
618→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
619→            return false
620→        }
621→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
622→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
623→        }
624→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
625→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
626→        }
627→
628→        var node: ASTNode? = this
629→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
630→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
631→                return false
632→            }
633→            node = node.nextSibling()
634→        }
635→        return true
636→    }

638→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
639→        node: ASTNode,
640→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
641→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
642→    ) {
643→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
644→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
645→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
646→                return
647→            }
648→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
649→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
650→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
651→            val wrapBlock =
652→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
653→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
654→                    )
655→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
656→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
657→                    endOfBlock,
658→                    autoCorrect,
659→                    emit,
660→                    node.treeParent.indent(),
661→                )
662→            }
663→        }
664→    }

666→    private companion object {
667→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
668→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
669→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
670→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
671→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
672→            ).toMap()
673→    }

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57→ *
 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72→     */
 73→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74→    /**
 75→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76→     */
 77→    private val code: String,
 78→    /**
 79→     * Providers of rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note
 80→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81→     */
 82→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83→    /**
 84→     * EditorConfig properties to be applied by default when linting/formatting the code.
 85→     */
 86→    private val editorConfigProperties: MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>,
 87→) {
 88→    private var filePath: String? = null
 89→    private var kotlinScript = false
 90→
 91→    /**
 92→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125→            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126→
127→        return this
128→    }
129→
130→    /**
131→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
132→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
133→     */
134→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        this.filePath = filePath
136→        return this
137→    }
138→
139→    /**
140→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
141→     */
142→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
143→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
144→        return this
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
149→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
150→     * indenting.
151→     *
152→     * Caution:
153→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
154→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
155→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
156→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
157→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
158→     *
159→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
160→     */
161→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
162→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
163→
164→        return this
165→    }
166→
167→    /**
168→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
169→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
170→     * indenting.
171→     *
172→     * Caution:
173→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
174→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
175→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
176→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
177→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
178→     *
179→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
180→     */
181→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProviders(vararg providers: (() -> Rule)): KtLintAssertThat {
182→        additionalRuleProviders.addAll(
183→            providers.map { RuleProvider(it) },
184→        )
185→
186→        return this
187→    }
188→
189→    /**
190→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
191→     *
192→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
193→     */
194→    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
195→
196→    /**
197→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
198→     */
199→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
200→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
201→
202→    /**
203→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
204→     *
205→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
206→     */
207→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
208→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
209→
210→    /**
211→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
212→     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
213→     */
214→    public fun hasLintViolation(
215→        line: Int,
216→        col: Int,
217→        detail: String,
218→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolation(line, col, detail)
219→
220→    /**
221→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected. Note that tests
222→     * resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
223→     */
224→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
225→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolations(*expectedErrors)
226→
227→    /**
228→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule which automatically can be
229→     * corrected. This is a sugar coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one
230→     * lint violation.
231→     */
232→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
233→        line: Int,
234→        col: Int,
235→        detail: String,
236→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(line, col, detail)
237→
238→    /**
239→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by an additional rules which can be
240→     * automatically corrected. Note that tests resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to
241→     * comprehend.
242→     */
243→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRule(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
244→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(*expectedErrors)
245→
246→    /**
247→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
248→     */
249→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
253→     */
254→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
255→        line: Int,
256→        col: Int,
257→        detail: String,
258→    ): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(line, col, detail)
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s which can not be automatically corrected. Note that
262→     * tests resulting in only one [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
263→     */
264→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): Unit =
265→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(*expectedErrors)
266→
267→    private fun ktLintAssertThatAssertable(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
268→        KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
269→            ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
270→            code = code(),
271→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigProperties.toEditorConfigOverride(),
272→            additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
273→        )
274→
275→    private fun MutableSet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>.toEditorConfigOverride() =
276→        if (isEmpty()) {
277→            EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
278→        } else {
279→            EditorConfigOverride.from(*toTypedArray())
280→        }
281→
282→    private fun code() =
283→        if (filePath != null) {
284→            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
285→            // the Ktlint test file system.
286→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
287→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
288→            Code.fromPath(path)
289→        } else {
290→            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)
291→        }
292→
293→    public companion object {
294→        /**
295→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
296→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules.
297→         */
298→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
299→
300→        /**
301→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has
302→         * extensions specifically for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only
303→         * executed during the format phase of the test. This means that the unit test only has to check the lint
304→         * violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But the code is formatted by both the rule
305→         * and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule definitions.
306→         */
307→
308→        public fun assertThatRule(
309→            provider: () -> Rule,
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
313→
314→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
315→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
316→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
317→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
318→            { code ->
319→                KtLintAssertThat(
320→                    ruleProvider = this,
321→                    code = code,
322→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
323→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
324→                )
325→            }
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
331→
332→        /**
333→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
334→         */
335→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
336→
337→        init {
338→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
339→             * val code =
340→             *     """
341→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
342→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
343→             *     """.trimIndent()
344→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
345→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
346→             */
347→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
348→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
349→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
350→            }
351→        }
352→    }
353→}

355→/**
356→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the
357→ * [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all
358→ * assertions operate on the same state.
359→ */
360→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
361→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
362→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
363→    private val code: Code,
364→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
365→    /**
366→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
367→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
368→     */
369→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
370→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
371→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
372→
373→    /**
374→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the
375→     * KtLintAssertThat.
376→     *
377→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
378→     */
379→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
380→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
381→
382→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
383→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
384→
385→        assertAll(
386→            {
387→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
388→                    .describedAs(
389→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
390→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
391→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
392→                            } else {
393→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
394→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
395→                            },
396→                    )
397→                    .isEmpty()
398→            },
399→            {
400→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
401→                    .describedAs(
402→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
403→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
404→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
405→                            } else {
406→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
407→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
408→                            },
409→                    )
410→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
411→            },
412→        )
413→    }
414→
415→    /**
416→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
417→     */
418→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
419→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
420→
421→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
422→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
423→            .isEmpty()
424→
425→        return this
426→    }
427→
428→    /**
429→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
430→     *
431→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly
432→     * formatted.
433→     *
434→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample
435→     * code, but the code is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each
436→     * other, a unit test cal still verify that code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not
437→     * violated.
438→     */
439→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
440→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
441→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
442→        }
443→
444→        val lintErrors = lint()
445→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
446→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
447→
448→        return this
449→    }
450→
451→    /**
452→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar coated version of
453→     * [hasLintViolation] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
454→     */
455→    public fun hasLintViolation(
456→        line: Int,
457→        col: Int,
458→        detail: String,
459→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
460→        hasLintViolations(
461→            LintViolation(
462→                line = line,
463→                col = col,
464→                detail = detail,
465→            ),
466→        )
467→
468→    /**
469→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar coated
470→     * version of [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
471→     */
472→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
473→        line: Int,
474→        col: Int,
475→        detail: String,
476→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
477→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
478→            LintViolation(
479→                line = line,
480→                col = col,
481→                detail = detail,
482→            ),
483→        )
484→
485→    /**
486→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
487→     */
488→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
489→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
490→
491→        val actualLintViolationFields =
492→            lint()
493→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
494→                .toLintViolationsFields()
495→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
496→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
497→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
498→        return this
499→    }
500→
501→    /**
502→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be
503→     * automatically corrected.
504→     */
505→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
506→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
507→
508→        val actualLintViolationFields =
509→            lint()
510→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
511→                .toLintViolationsFields()
512→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
513→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
514→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields(canBeAutoCorrected = true))
515→        return this
516→    }
517→
518→    /**
519→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
520→     */
521→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
522→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
523→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
524→        }
525→
526→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
527→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
528→
529→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
530→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
531→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
532→
533→        return this
534→    }
535→
536→    /**
537→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
538→     */
539→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
540→        line: Int,
541→        col: Int,
542→        detail: String,
543→    ): Unit =
544→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
545→            LintViolation(
546→                line = line,
547→                col = col,
548→                detail = detail,
549→            ),
550→        )
551→
552→    /**
553→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically
554→     * corrected.
555→     */
556→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {

594→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {

597→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of
598→                // the additional rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
599→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)

603→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)

656→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
657→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =

659→            .asSequence()
660→            .map { ruleProvider ->

666→            }.distinct()

668→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
669→            .toList()
670→            .toTypedArray()
671→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
